title: helianthemum polifolium proles pilosum
intro: Helianthemum polifolium proles pilosum, also known as rockrose, is a perennial shrub native to the western United States. It belongs to the cistus or rockrose family, and is valued for its medicinal and ornamental properties. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers are rich in flavonoids, triterpenoids, and other bioactive compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including wounds, digestive problems, and respiratory infections. Rockrose can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is also used in herbal remedies, such as salves and ointments, to promote wound healing and soothe skin irritations.
